vocab.meaning: cảm giác tự do tự tại
vocab.example: I prefer being a driver because it provides a sense of liberation that you just don't get when you're stuck in the passenger seat.

vocab.meaning: đa nhiệm một cách hiệu quả (vừa đi vừa làm việc khác)
vocab.example: Being a passenger allows me to multitask effectively, whether that's catching up on emails or simply preparing for my next meeting.

vocab.meaning: sự mệt mỏi do chuyến đi dài bằng ô tô
vocab.example: I'd much rather be a passenger to avoid road-trip fatigue, especially when traveling across long, monotonous stretches of highway.

vocab.meaning: sự thỏa mãn về mặt vận động/cảm giác cơ thể
vocab.example: There's a certain kinesthetic satisfaction in shifting gears and perfectly timing your braking that I find deeply rewarding.

vocab.meaning: sự mệt mỏi về mặt nhận thức/đầu óc
vocab.example: After a long day at work, I prefer to be chauffeured so I don't succumb to cognitive fatigue while navigating heavy traffic.

vocab.meaning: sự làm chủ việc vận hành (xe)
vocab.example: I enjoy the feeling of operational mastery over a powerful machine; it makes the commute far more engaging.

vocab.meaning: chỉ là một người quan sát thuần túy
vocab.example: I find it a bit dull to be a mere spectator during a long journey, so I always volunteer to take the first shift behind the wheel.

vocab.meaning: sự nhận thức tình huống được nâng cao
vocab.example: Driving demands a heightened situational awareness that keeps me focused and mentally sharp throughout the trip.

vocab.meaning: vùng đệm chống lại sự căng thẳng
vocab.example: For me, having someone else drive acts as a buffer against stress, allowing me to arrive at my destination feeling refreshed.

vocab.meaning: những thao tác điều khiển phức tạp
vocab.example: I take pride in executing intricate maneuvers in tight spaces, which is why I'll always choose to drive over being a passenger.
